steps.title: Visualize the unit circle setup
steps.content:
$$G = (0, 0),\quad F = (1, 0),\quad H = (-1, y)$$
Since all points lie on a unit circle, distances $GF = GH = 1$. The angle $FGH$ is formed at the center between radii $\overrightarrow{GF}$ and $\overrightarrow{GH}$.

steps.title: Apply central angle properties
steps.content:
The central angle between points with coordinates $x = 1\to -1$ on the unit circle spans a semicircle ($\pi$ radians). For $H = (-1, y)$ to lie on the circle, solving $x^2 + y^2 = 1$:
$$(-1)^2 + y^2 = 1 \Rightarrow y = 0$$
Thus, $\angle FGH$ is the central angle between diametrically opposite points $F$ and $H$.

steps.title: Account for multiple full rotations
steps.content:
The positive measure of $\angle FGH$ can include full rotations ($2\pi$) added to $\pi$. Check which choice equals $\pi + 2\pi k$:
  • Divide each choice by $\pi$ and subtract even integers

Only $35\pi$ simplifies to $\pi$ after subtracting $34\pi$, matching the central angle requirement.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D represents $17$ full rotations plus the base angle $\pi$.D is correct.
